label: 850_savers_low rates_draghi_bild
description: The European Central Bank's prolonged low-interest-rate policy faced criticism from German savers. ECB President Mario Draghi defended the measures, explaining how they aimed to support the economy and combat deflationary pressures, while acknowledging potential drawbacks such as reduced effectiveness in highly indebted economies.

earliest_articles.title: Benoît Coeuré: Savers aren't losing out
earliest_articles.excerpt: Benoît Coeuré: Savers aren't losing out Complaints about the European Central Bank (ECB) favouring borrowers over savers with its low-interest-rate policy are getting ever louder. In countries such as Germany there is even talk of a "cold expropriation" of those who save money for their old age. I don't think this…
